items.title: Kubernetes v1.36: Mutable Pod Resources for Suspended Jobs (beta)
items.summary:
<p>Kubernetes v1.36 promotes the ability to modify container resource requests and limits
in the pod template of a suspended Job to beta. First introduced as alpha in v1.35, this
feature allows queue controllers and cluster administrators to adjust CPU, memory, GPU,
and extended resource specific
items.reason: Kubernetes v1.36 introduces a beta feature (Mutable Pod Resources for Suspended Jobs) that is a notable change in workload management. Highly relevant for a Kubernetes documentation section.

items.title: Kubernetes v1.36: Fine-Grained Kubelet API Authorization Graduates to GA
items.summary:
<p>On behalf of Kubernetes SIG Auth and SIG Node, we are pleased to announce the
graduation of fine-grained <code>kubelet</code> API authorization to General Availability
(GA) in Kubernetes v1.36!</p>
<p>The <code>KubeletFineGrainedAuthz</code> feature gate was introduced as an
items.reason: Fine‑grained Kubelet API authorization graduating to GA is a security‑related milestone for Kubernetes and warrants its own wiki page.

items.title: Kubernetes v1.36: User Namespaces in Kubernetes are finally GA
items.summary:
<p>After several years of development, User Namespaces support in
Kubernetes reached General Availability (GA) with the v1.36 release.
This is a Linux-only feature.</p>
<p>For those of us working on low level container runtimes and rootless
technologies, this has been a long awaited milesto
items.reason: User Namespaces reaching GA is a major feature for container isolation and rootless operation; essential content for a Kubernetes security/operations guide.

items.title: SELinux Volume Label Changes goes GA (and likely implications in v1.37)
items.summary:
<p>If you run Kubernetes on Linux with SELinux in enforcing mode, plan ahead: a future release (anticipated to be v1.37) is
expected to turn the <code>SELinuxMount</code> <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/command-line-tools-reference/feature-gates/">feature gate</a> on by
items.reason: SELinux volume label changes and the upcoming default enablement affect Linux‑based clusters; relevant for Kubernetes SELinux documentation.

items.title: Trivy, KICS, and the shape of supply chain attacks so far in 2026
items.summary: Catching the KICS push: what happened, and the case for open, fast collaboration In the past few weeks we've worked through two supply chain compromises on Docker Hub with a similar shape: first Trivy, now Checkmarx KICS. In both cases, stolen publisher credentials were used to push malicious images
items.reason: Supply‑chain attacks involving Trivy and KICS are critical security topics for Docker users; merit a dedicated wiki entry.

items.title: Why MicroVMs: The Architecture Behind Docker Sandboxes
items.summary: Last week, we launched Docker Sandboxes with a bold goal: to deliver the strongest agent isolation in the market. This post unpacks that claim, how microVMs enable it, and some of the architectural choices we made in this approach. The Problem With Every Other Approach Every sandboxing model asks yo
items.reason: MicroVM‑based Docker Sandboxes introduce a new isolation model; important for Docker architecture documentation.

items.title: Why We Chose the Harder Path: Docker Hardened Images, One Year Later
items.summary: We're coming up on a year since launching Docker Hardened Images (DHI) last May, and crossing a milestone earlier this month made me stop and reflect on what we've actually been building. Earlier this month, we crossed over 500k daily pulls of DHIs, and over 25k continuously patched OS level artifac
items.reason: One‑year retrospective on Docker Hardened Images provides usage statistics and lessons learned; valuable for Docker security best‑practices.

items.title: oven-sh/bun bun-v1.3.13
items.summary:
To install Bun v1.3.13
```bash
curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash
# or you can use npm
# npm install -g bun
```

Windows:
```bash
powershell -c "irm bun.sh/install.ps1|iex"
```

To upgrade to Bun v1.3.13:
```bash
bun upgrade
```

### [Read Bun v1.3.13's release notes on Bun's
items.reason: Bun v1.3.13 release provides installation and upgrade instructions; relevant for a JavaScript runtime comparison page.
